title: moricandia arvensis subsp. suffruticosa
intro: Moricandia arvensis subsp. suffruticosa, also known as wild radish or jointed charlock, is a plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its edible root and leafy greens. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Moricandia arvensis subsp. suffruticosa include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as acting as an antimicrobial agent. Moricandia arvensis subsp. suffruticosa can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as tea, infusion, and salve, to harness its medicinal properties.
